title: Annoying bookmark toolbar bug when enabling vertical tabs
content:
Switched on vertical tabs in the latest Firefox release (149.0.2) and noticed a bit of an annoying issue with the bookmark toolbar. If setted only on new tabs - it push all ui of vertical tabs down, and when you switch - it's going up again. I’ve attached a GIF to show what I mean.

I’ve enabled the option to show the bookmark bar only on new tabs, along with the vertical tabs setting. This behavior doesn’t seem right to me — I’d expect the bookmark bar to sit beside the vertical tab sidebar without shifting it. The current design is pretty frustrating.

Is there any way I can fix this? I think it's a clear bug in UI. Thanks!
creator: Anilexis

title: Cannot sign in with email previously used as secondary — "Primary account email required for sign-in"
content:
Hi,

I'm unable to sign in or create a new Firefox account with my email address hyeok@cv-3.com. I keep getting the error: "Primary account email required for sign-in."

Here's what happened: 1. I previously added hyeok@cv-3.com as a secondary email on my primary Mozilla account (ground4ekd@gmail.com). 2. I later removed it from the secondary email list. 3. The secondary email section on my account settings now shows "None." 4. However, when I try to sign in or register a new account with hyeok@cv-3.com, I still get the "Primary account email required" error.

It appears that Mozilla's server still associates hyeok@cv-3.com with my primary account even though it has been removed. Could you please fully disassociate hyeok@cv-3.com from ground4ekd@gmail.com on the server side so that I can use it independently?

I'd like to be able to use hyeok@cv-3.com as an independent account. Any help would be appreciated.

Thank you.
creator: ground4ekd

title: DNS over HTTPS problem.
content: A while ago when using the setting firefox noticed that i was using a vpn and turned the settings off. At least that was what i believed. Turns out, that only works with browser vpn addons that use the firefox settings. But these addons do not use wireguard, but tls to encrypt your data. If i use a vpn, and want to use the dns settings provided by them, i have to turn of the feature the dns over https feature from firefox. I only want to use that feature, if im not "protected" by a vpn solution of my choice.
